name: August Smart Lock
short_description: A retrofit smart deadbolt that clips onto an existing lock and adds app control, auto-unlock, and door-state sensing.
long_description: The August Smart Lock mounts on the interior side of a standard deadbolt and connects over Bluetooth and a Wi-Fi bridge to a smartphone app, letting people lock and unlock remotely, grant virtual guest keys, and see a log of who comes and goes. Its auto-unlock feature uses phone geofencing and proximity to open the door as the owner arrives, while DoorSense and intelligent auto-lock detect whether the door is actually closed and secure it automatically. As an ambient, infrastructure-embedded system that senses context and acts on the owner's behalf, it is an everyday example of physical AI quietly making decisions about a home's threshold rather than waiting for an explicit command.

images.caption: The second-generation August smart lock mounted on a deadbolt, in an unlocked state.
images.alt_text: A circular silver August smart lock unit attached to the interior thumb-turn of a residential door deadbolt.

sources.title: August Wi-Fi Smart Lock
sources.access_notes: Official product page describing auto-lock, auto-unlock, DoorSense, and app features.

sources.title: August Home
sources.access_notes: Company history; first August Smart Lock released May 2013, founded 2012 by Yves Behar and Jason Johnson.

interactions.title: The door opens before you reach it
interactions.description: Walk up with both arms full of groceries and the deadbolt turns itself as your phone's geofence registers that you're home, so you never set the bags down to fish for keys.

interactions.title: It locks the second the door clicks shut
interactions.description: DoorSense feels the door settle into the frame and throws the bolt instantly, so the lock distinguishes a genuinely closed door from one left ajar instead of blindly locking on a timer.

interactions.title: Ask out loud if you locked up
interactions.description: From bed you ask Alexa, Siri, or Google whether the front door is locked, and unlock by voice after speaking a PIN — no phone in hand, no trip downstairs.

interactions.title: Guests let themselves in while you're away
interactions.description: Airbnb hosts wire the lock to the booking so each guest's code goes live only for the dates of their stay, and the same panel hands a recurring code to the cleaner between bookings.

interactions.title: Unlocking the door starts the house
interactions.description: People route the lock through IFTTT so that turning the bolt fires a chain reaction: Hue lights come up, the ecobee shifts to Home, the garage closes — the lock becoming the trigger for an arrival routine August never shipped.

interactions.title: The door that unlocks itself for no one
interactions.description: Owners report the bolt occasionally springing open on its own — usually GPS drift or a geofence overlapping a neighbor's tripping auto-unlock — turning a convenience feature into the most unnerving thing the lock can do.

interactions.title: A quiet ping when the kid gets home
interactions.description: Tie a notification to a named family key so a parent gets a discreet alert the moment that specific person unlocks the door after school — built from the per-user activity feed the lock already keeps.

interactions.title: Flag the day the pattern breaks
interactions.description: Let the lock learn the household's usual lock-and-leave rhythm from its own log and surface a gentle nudge when something is off — the door left unlocked past the time it's normally secured, or opened at an hour no one is usually home.
